content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Attached please find the business update for SCS, Lovito, and warehouse for
our discussion on Oct 21, focusing on the 2026 growth plan.

Thank you!

Best,
Chen Gang

Hi Ian,

Attached pls find the latest discussion material for tomorrow's meeting,
with added sections on product update.

Let us know if you have any questions. Thanks!

Best,
Gang

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 12

   - Can indicate the expected ADO of each of this initiative here?

Slide 13

   - I remember we already had a similar initiative at the category level
   where we were going straight to factories?
How is this different?
- How are we measuring our COGS over time?

To ensure that for top
   selling SKUs, the prices that we are bringing them in for is trending
   downwards?
Especially when we explore factory suppliers?

Slide 16

   - Quite a few of these brands are already on Shopee?

It is not so clear
   to me what we bring to them that they do not already have?

Slide 22

   - What is the definition of high ASP here?
- How does this compare with top CB sellers?

For IOR as well as high ASP
   contribution?
- For high ASP, which category will they fall in on Slide 12?

Slide 23

   - Similar question on bundle contribution



Slide 33

   - What is this official WH capacity limits issue we are referring to?
- BS% is not calculated as a % of GMV right?

But as a % of stock in
   local WH?
- I am not sure about this strategy?

Our LFF% is way higher than other
   CB sellers?
Which means that our BS cost will probably escalate?

- Not quite sure what we are saving for here in general, cannot quite
   understand the calculations



Slide 38

   - What is the % of Fashion ADO and ADGMV that we intend to contribute?

Slide 43

   - What is the expected contribution that IP collaboration will be of our
   business in ’26?

Slide 45

   - Are we doing any co-brand on accessories front?

For eg. for bags
   similar to Labubu craze?

Seems good to match with some of the IP
   collaborations we are going after?

Slide 50

   - What is this exactly?

I remember we wanted to expand our bag
   assortment. But we want to open a separate store?

Slide 52

   - I cannot reconcile the ADO uplift with the table above
   - Why are we not scaling this faster?

Is there a pitch deck that we can
   send to the country heads?

We are already spending so much on KOL in each
   market on a monthly basis, surely we can set aside some money for this?

Slide 61

   - Similar comments as above, how does this tie in with our BS %?

Not
   sure we are just putting for the sake of putting



Others

   - I remember we wanted to scale other sub-cats, how is that doing?
- I remember we wanted to scale refine, how is that doing?
- How are the Video/LS contribution coming along?
- How about AMS?

How is our contribution compared to other Fashion
   sellers right now?

Hi Ian,

Attached please find the updated deck for your review, with key changes
highlighted below

For SCS

   -

   Page 4: in Executive summary, we’ve added key message for new assortment
   planning
   -

      New assortment (ADO +67K / ADG + 129K ): we plan to grow each
      sub-group of assortment via major initiatives, delivering SCS values
      -

         Short tail (cheaper, faster and better quality): continue improve
         COGS capability by onboarding low-priced factories from 源头产业带
         -

         Long tail (cheaper and better quality): enhance supplier response
         time and build “supplier direct-to-SOC” capability in JIT fulfillment
         -

         Brand on Shopee (cheaper and faster): continue to optimize our
         COGS/pricing management and brand operation capabilities
         -

         Brand Not on Shopee (diversity): bring CN brands NOT on Shopee
         (rejected MP model) or with different offerings on Shopee vs. CN
         -

         Non-brand & NOT on Shopee (diversity): introduce competitor
         hot-selling models to Shopee via targeted sourcing
         -

   Page 6: we’ve added SCS/CB ADO and ADG% targets
   -

      SCS/CB ADO% remain flat at 7.7%
      -

      SCS/CB ADG% will increase from 3.3% (Aug’25) to 4.2% (Dec’26)
      -

   Page 7: similarly, we’ve added by quarter and full year SCS/CB ADO and
   ADG% targets
   -

   Page 8: New, added for monthly P&L breakdown to show that we will reach
   break-even by end of 2026 even after returning SLS discount and Ads free
   credits
   -

    (major change) Page 11: Updated new assortment sub-group planning
   summary
   -

   Page 32-33: Updated LFF acceleration/BS/Resell optimization message and
   calculation for ease of understanding and back-up page for monthly breakdown


For Lovito

   -

   Page 36: in Executive summary, we’ve updated key message for assortment
   planning
